title: galarhoeus ibericus
intro: Galarhoeus ibericus, also known as Iberis amara or bitter candytuft, is a plant species within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and bitter taste. Traditionally, Galarhoeus ibericus has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate respiratory issues and reduce pain. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Galarhoeus ibericus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
